title: Use k6 extensions in Grafana Cloud k6

blocks.content: **What you'll learn:** How to run a Grafana Cloud k6 test that imports a supported extension (using `k6/x/faker` as an example), execute it, and confirm the extension output, without building a custom k6 binary.

blocks.title: Run a test with the faker extension

blocks.content: You'll open a k6 project, start a scripted test that uses an extension, run it, and check **Logs** for the faker output.
k6 extensions extend core k6 behavior. In k6 open source you can build a custom binary; in Grafana Cloud k6 you can import a supported subset in your scripts, without any custom build required.
**k6 test with extensions** — Go to the k6 projects page, then follow the steps below.
Click **Create new test** to create a sample k6 test with extensions.
Add this script: it imports **k6/x/faker** and logs a random first name when the test runs.
Click **Create and Run** and wait for the test to finish.
Open the **Logs** tab to confirm the extension ran.

You learned how to import **k6/x/faker**, run the test in Grafana Cloud k6, and verify extension output on **Logs** (you should see a random first name). For the full list of supported extensions, see [Use k6 extensions](https://grafana.com/docs/grafana-cloud/testing/k6/author-run/use-k6-extensions/#import-an-extension-in-your-test-script).
